title: Ipomoea bona-nox
intro: Ipomoea bona-nox, commonly known as Convolvulus, is a plant species in the morning glory family that contains psychoactive and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Ipomoea bona-nox include its ability to act as an anxiolytic and sedative, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of insomnia and other sleep disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids such as ergine, lysergic acid, and ergonovine, which contribute to its medicinal and psychoactive effects. Traditionally, Ipomoea bona-nox has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other conditions related to stress and sleep.
